latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
30 Apr 2026 16:30
RNS Number : 6276C
Vanquis Banking Group PLC
30 April 2026
Vanquis Banking Group plc - Voting Rights and Capital
In accordance with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1., Vanquis Banking Group plc hereby gives notice that as of 30 April 2026, the company's issued share capital consists of 256,493,758 ordinary shares of 20 8/11p each, with voting rights. Vanquis Banking Group plc does not hold any ordinary shares in Treasury.
The above figure 256,493,758 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, Vanquis Banking Group plc under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Elizabeth McClure
Deputy Company Secretary
Tel: 01274 351 351

latest.title: Motor Finance Redress Schemes Update
latest.content:
27 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9226B
Vanquis Banking Group PLC
27 April 2026
Vanquis Banking Group plc ("the Company")
Motor Finance Redress Schemes Update
27 April 2026:
Vanquis Banking Group plc ("the Group" or "Vanquis") confirms that it will not challenge the Financial Conduct Authority's Motor Finance Redress Schemes and is focused on implementation.
The Group did not participate in discretionary commission arrangements or operate tied selling arrangements and is therefore not in scope for these elements of the Schemes.
The previously disclosed £3.0 million provision in respect of this matter remains unchanged.
The Group remains committed to ensuring appropriate redress to customers where loss has occurred.
Vanquis intends to release its 1Q26 trading statement on 6 May.
Enquiries
Analysts and shareholders
James Cranstoun, Head of Investor Relations
james.cranstoun@vanquis.com
+44 (0) 7766 937 406

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
8 Apr 2026 11:00
RNS Number : 5207Z
Vanquis Banking Group PLC
08 April 2026
Vanquis Banking Group plc ("the Company")
Director/PDMR Transaction - Initial Notification
The Company announces that it has granted the following awards on 7 April 2026
over the Company's ordinary shares of 20 8/11p ("Shares") as follows:
1. nil-cost options over 646,976 and 367,879 Shares for no consideration to Ian McLaughlin and David Watts respectively, who are directors/persons discharging managerial responsibility ("PDMRs") in the Company, under the 2020 Restricted Share Plan (the "RSP Options")
2. awards over 95,595 and 60,434 Shares to Ian McLaughlin and David Watts respectively, who are directors and PDMRs in the Company, under the Deferred Bonus Plan 2022 (the "DBP Awards").
The number of ordinary shares awarded under the RSP Options and DBP Awards is based on the average closing share price on the London Stock Exchange between 27 March 2026 and 2 April 2026 of £1.1376.
No adjustments have been made to the grant value of the awards
.
A further assessment will be made at the point of vest as part of the RSP performance underpin. There has been no additional discount from the price at grant to reflect the absence of dividend equivalents during the vesting period and no market value options linked to the RSP Options have been granted under the 2020 Restricted Share Plan (CSOP Options).
In respect of the DBP Awards, JTC Trust Company (CI) Limited, will transfer the beneficial interest to the participants, including the PDMRs, in satisfaction of the DBP Awards granted subject to and in accordance with the terms of the Deferred Bonus Plan 2022.

company_30s.what_they_do: Vanquis is a UK‑based consumer finance group that lends to people with limited credit history.  It offers short‑term loans, credit cards, and motor‑finance products.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The sub‑prime lending space is still sizeable in the UK, and Vanquis has a niche advantage: a large existing customer base that prefers quick, online credit.  Its motor‑finance arm is a key revenue driver, giving it exposure to the auto‑market’s rebound after the pandemic slowdown.
company_30s.current_state: The business is in a tight squeeze – it’s been trading at a fraction of its historical highs and has been tightening its balance sheet.  The price is near a 90‑plus percent drop from peak, signalling a deep bottom that has already seen most of the weak hands exit.

rns_narrative: Vanquis has been busy on the governance front – new directors, updated voting rights, and a clearer picture of its shareholding structure.  These moves suggest the board is tightening control and preparing for a more transparent capital structure.

The motor‑finance redress scheme update shows the company is actively managing its regulatory obligations and protecting customer interests, which could improve its reputation with both regulators and borrowers.
The latest earnings release and AGM notice hint at a coming presentation that could reveal fresh funding plans or a strategic pivot to a higher‑margin product mix.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the chatter is almost entirely bullish, driven by analyst coverage and upcoming events.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Berenberg’s new ‘buy’ rating and a 165p target, plus the upcoming Mello Birmingham presentation, paint a picture of a company on the verge of a turnaround.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Sub‑prime lenders face tightening regulation and higher default rates; some investors worry about the company’s debt load and the sustainability of its current cash flow.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from passive indifference to active bullishness after the analyst endorsement, but the overall volume remains low – a classic contrarian signal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning the market is largely unaware of Vanquis.  A surprise catalyst could trigger a big move.

near_term_catalysts: The February 26 earnings report – a beat on earnings per share could ignite a rebound.
The AGM on March 4 – if the board announces a new funding round or a strategic partnership, it could lift sentiment.
The Mello Birmingham presentation on April 22 – a pitch of a new product line or a partnership with a major automaker could be a trigger.
Any sign of a volume spike around key support levels (roughly 30p) would confirm that buyers are stepping in.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for the stock to break above the 30p support with at least 10‑fold average daily volume.  A clear uptick in the 200‑day moving average and a return of the 50‑day to 200‑day crossover would validate the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still in the early stages of a deep bottom – most of the weak hands have sold.  The APEX score shows a tight compression, indicating that a breakout could be imminent once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down more than 85% from its peak, giving it a massive upside if it recovers to the 50% level of its historical range.  A 30p rebound would represent roughly a 200% gain from the current price, and the 165p target from Berenberg is a realistic next step.  With a win‑rate of 56.9% for deep crashes, the probability of a large bounce is high.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure fresh capital or its sub‑prime portfolio continues to deteriorate, the price could slide further into the 20p range.  The debt‑to‑EBITDA ratio is still high, and regulatory scrutiny could add pressure.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30p (current price) to 20p (worst case) – a 33% drop.  Potential gain: 30p to 165p – a 450% upside.  The asymmetry is roughly 1:15, which is attractive for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above 30p with a 3‑day volume surge.  Size the position so that a 10p drop from entry would hit your 2% risk limit.  Place a stop just below the 25p support to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the earnings release or AGM announcement.  If the company announces new funding or a partnership, enter at the next price high that confirms the breakout, keeping the stop below 25p.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted, files for bankruptcy, or a major regulator imposes a penalty that forces it out of business, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Vanquis sits at a deep crash bottom with a clear, upcoming catalyst and a highly asymmetric upside – a prime contrarian play if the company can secure fresh capital or a new partnership.
